value.tech_stack: Next.js 16 / TypeScript / React 18 / Tailwind / shadcn/ui / React Flow / AI SDK / JWT / PostgreSQL

bugs.description: 32/44 API routes have NO authentication — middleware bypasses all /api/ routes
CI pipeline does not run tests — undetected regressions shipping to prod
TypeScript strict mode disabled — masking type safety bugs across codebase
No runtime input validation (Zod schemas missing on POST/PUT routes)
20+ stale markdown docs and debug scripts cluttering project root
Duplicate middleware files (root vs src/middleware.ts) — confusion risk

risks: Unauthenticated API access in production — data exposure / manipulation risk
Demo-stage features exposed publicly could break during client demos
No preview environments — all QA is manual before demos
Oracle ML and vectorization agents have untested failure modes
Low test coverage (24 tests for 25 pages + 30 components + 44 API routes)
strengths: Strong recent improvements: ReactFlow v12 migration, Next.js CVE patches, dead code cleanup
Comprehensive feature set: 25 pages, AI chat, visual editor, Oracle ML, admin console, Salesforce embed
JWT auth with CSRF double-submit pattern implemented
GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline exists (lint + build)
High strategic value — core enterprise product for ForwardLane/SignalHaus
recommendations: P0: Add requireAuth() middleware to all 32 unprotected API routes immediately
P0: Fix CI pipeline to run tests, add Playwright smoke tests, enforce coverage thresholds
P1: Enable TypeScript strict mode and fix resulting type errors
P1: Add Zod schema validation on all POST/PUT API routes
P2: Archive stale root docs, increase test coverage to 60%+
P3: Add API documentation (OpenAPI), implement feature flags

value.tech_stack: React 18 / Vite / TypeScript / Redux Toolkit / React Query / Axios / Storybook / nginx

bugs.description: Bitbucket pipeline uses REACT_APP_* env vars instead of VITE_* — deployed builds cannot connect to API
Axios response interceptor returns error object instead of Promise.reject — React Query errors silently swallowed
localStorage auth token storage — XSS vulnerability, should use httpOnly cookies
Storybook uses webpack bundler alongside Vite — unnecessary bloat and confusing
Vite config has typo alias @proccesses alongside @processes
Duplicate Navigation export in shared/widgets/index.ts
@types/react-router v5 types conflicting with react-router-dom v6

risks: Deployed demo/QA environments completely broken due to env var prefix mismatch
Silent API error swallowing means users get no feedback on failures
XSS attack could steal auth tokens from localStorage
~15-20% test coverage — regressions ship undetected
nginx config missing critical security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options)
strengths: Successful CRA→Vite migration completed (10x faster builds)
Redux Toolkit + React Query architecture is modern and well-structured
Sentry error monitoring integrated
Clean Feature-Sliced Design architecture
Active CI pipeline exists in Bitbucket
recommendations: P0: Fix Bitbucket pipeline env vars: rename all REACT_APP_* to VITE_* (immediate — prod is broken)
P0: Fix Axios interceptor to return Promise.reject(error) instead of error object
P1: Remove localStorage auth token fallback — use httpOnly cookies only
P1: Add Playwright E2E tests for critical flows
P1: Add nginx security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options)
P2: Migrate Storybook to Vite builder, increase test coverage to 50%+

value.tech_stack: Node.js 20 / Express 4 / Sequelize 3 / MySQL / Jade / Gulp / PM2 / Passport / RabbitMQ / AWS SDK v2

bugs.description: Sequelize 3.x has multiple known CVEs and is unmaintained
Watson NLC dead code references still scattered across 10+ files despite LLM gateway replacement
express-validator 2.x severely outdated with known issues
Duplicate Morningstar integration (morning_star/ AND morningstar_funds/ directories)
lodash 4.17.4 needs update to 4.17.21+ for prototype pollution fixes
Test rig config files in production config directory
WATSON_DIALOG_PASSWORD env var still referenced in config/express.js

risks: Sequelize 3 → 6+ upgrade is a massive breaking change affecting 40+ models
Legacy Node.js 0.10 patterns still pervasive despite v20 upgrade
Near-zero test coverage — any change risks breaking undocumented behavior
50+ npm audit vulnerabilities expected
AWS SDK v2 reaching end of life
Jade template engine deprecated (renamed Pug), no security patches
strengths: Rich domain model: 40+ Sequelize models covering clients, portfolios, instruments, strategies, recommendations, alerts
Recent significant improvements: Node 20 upgrade, LLM gateway replacing Watson NLC, SSH exfil removal, security hardening
63 unit tests added for core adviser logic
Extensive feature set: 20+ route modules covering full financial advisor workflow
Strategic IP value — contains core ForwardLane business logic and domain knowledge
recommendations: P0: Remove all remaining Watson dead code (10+ files with NLC references)
P0: Add streaming LLM dialog features
P1: Upgrade Sequelize 3→6 with migration testing (biggest risk/reward item)
P1: Upgrade AWS SDK v2→v3, implement Redis session store
P1: Achieve 40%+ test coverage, set up CI/CD pipeline
P2: Merge duplicate Morningstar directories, add rate limiting and health checks
Decision point: Consider extracting domain logic for modern rebuild vs incremental upgrade

value.tech_stack: TypeScript / Express / Genkit / Claude / Gemini / fal.ai / SQLite / Stripe / Docker / Railway

bugs.description: tenants.ts uses better-sqlite3 directly, bypassing WAL singleton in db.ts — lock contention on every API auth call
SHA-256 API key hashing is brute-forceable — should use scrypt
Wildcard genkit dependencies (^*) are a breaking-change time bomb
Unreachable service trendpilotBridge.ts — no route consumer found
@types/better-sqlite3 in prod dependencies instead of devDependencies
Full lodash import (~70KB) instead of modular imports

risks: Dual SQLite connections can cause database locks under concurrent tenant auth + content operations
Weak API key hashing allows brute-force if database file is compromised
Unpinned genkit deps could break builds on any upstream release
No frontend — product is API-only, limiting non-technical user adoption
Content scheduling engine exists but execution reliability needs validation
strengths: Strongest test suite in T2 set: 287 tests, integration tests, CI/CD pipeline
Multi-tenant Stripe billing already implemented
Comprehensive AI pipeline: content generation, video, audio/podcasts, brand management, campaigns
SQLite migration system with proper WAL mode (mostly)
OpenAPI documentation in place
Docker + Railway deployment configured
Solid architecture with Genkit flows, quota enforcement, smart auth
recommendations: P0: Migrate tenants.ts to centralized db.ts singleton (eliminate dual SQLite connections)
P0: Replace SHA-256 hashing with scrypt for API keys
P0: Pin wildcard genkit dependencies to fixed versions
P1: Add SQLite indexes on hot columns, implement pino structured logging
P2: Build React dashboard frontend for content pipeline management
P2: Implement LRU cache for AI generation flows to reduce API costs

React 19 frontend with glassmorphic UI

Clear B2C SaaS potential with $10-30/mo subscription model

P1: Deploy to Railway with PostgreSQL + Redis, configure Twilio webhook
